question.text: A listed company wants to buy back 6% of its paid-up capital through the open market via stock exchange after April 1, 2025. Is this permitted?

answer.text: No, open market buyback through stock exchange is not allowed from April 1, 2025
Yes, since open market buyback is always allowed for listed companies
Yes, but only if done through the book building method
Yes, if the buyback amount is less than 10% of paid-up capital

explanation.text: **The Logic:** From April 1, 2025, buyback through the open market via stock exchange is NOT ALLOWED. Prior to this date, open market buyback of 5% or more of paid-up capital was already prohibited. After April 1, 2025, the prohibition is absolute for stock exchange route. **The Trap:** "Yes, but only if done through the book building method" might confuse candidates because book building was an alternative open market method, but the post-April 2025 prohibition covers stock exchange buybacks entirely. **Cross-Exam Context:** This regulatory change is a recent amendment tested specifically in NISM Series IX; awareness of regulatory evolution is key. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Post April 2025, the only route for buyback is the tender offer method — restructure your advisory practice accordingly.
